judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with grounded evidence; they are comparable, with Report 2 slightly crisper on the AI-footer removal.

report_1.evidence: "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.` `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... this section must be corrected to "opt-in" or removed. ... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` returning an empty list and the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. ... The entire "Export your visit history" section must be removed.

report_2.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (line 24) — remove or rewrite as upcoming.** The feature does not exist. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` endpoint returning `[]` and the comment `# TODO: pdf export`... There is no export route anywhere ... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30) — false, correct or remove.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_1.evidence: `docs/release/captures.json` records `before_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/schedule` but `after_scheduling.png` from route `/billing/invoices`. ... the "after" screenshot is a different screen entirely, not the redesigned calendar

report_2.evidence: `docs/release/captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured on route `/billing/invoices` (while `before_scheduling.png` was `/schedule`), and rendering the image confirms it: it visibly reads "BILLING / INVOICES"

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png` ... referenced at `highlights-v4.md` line 26 but the file does not exist ... The email would ship a broken image. | `unused_capture_07.png` | **no** | yes | orphan file showing "PATIENT LIST"; captured but referenced nowhere

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` (doc line 26) does not exist.** No such file is in the repo ... The email would ship with a broken image ... `unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere** in the document

report_1.evidence: The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33). Two problems: the word "draft" itself signals unfinished internal material and should never appear in the sent version, and the AI-attribution line is an attribution artifact

report_2.evidence: The trailing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33)** is an internal drafting note. It should not appear in a polished customer email ... remove it after doing the corrections above
